title: Modern Silver Rosary Necklace — Commercial Product Package (Hero Retouch + Vector Spec + Screen-Print Tech Graphic + Data-Merge Spec Sheet)
one_line_ask: Turn the client's raw studio shot of a modern silver rosary necklace into a full commercial product package: a retouched hero image on a clean sweep, a vector line-art spec, a screen-print-style halftone tech graphic, and a print-ready data-merged spec sheet — all assembled into a review board for revision rounds.

issues: Very faint vertical seam visible through the center, likely from export or compositing, but not severe for a source asset.
Background is transparent as required, but the checkerboard pattern is visible (expected for transparency preview, not an issue for the asset itself).
Minor alignment/spacing tweaks could be made in final design, but acceptable for a source file.
Cross glyph is slightly asymmetric — vertical arm is notably longer than horizontal, giving it a more elongated crucifix feel rather than a balanced line-cross; minor but worth refining
Kerning between '&' and 'FAITH' appears very slightly tighter than the rest of the wordmark — barely perceptible but a designer should check
Logo sits left-of-center in the frame rather than being perfectly centered — composition is slightly unbalanced for a lockup asset
Very slight warm/yellow color cast from desk lamp (intentional per brief, needs color temperature correction in post)
Chain coils slightly off-center toward upper right quadrant (minor composition note, acceptable for candid brief)
Background includes wooden soldering block and metal tweezers/tools visible (intentional clutter per brief, needs isolation in post)
Mild luminance noise visible in grey workbench surface (intentional per brief)
Slight tilt/crooked framing (intentional per brief, needs auto-straighten)
Shallow depth of field causes upper chain loops to go slightly soft (intentional per brief)

assets.name: spec_sheet_template.indd
assets.note: Client-provided .indd template; represented here as an authored text proxy (binary Adobe format not synthesizable).
